title: kafka-skill-creator manifest (v1)
description: Declares the Kafka topics, consumer groups, cluster shape, and per-environment contexts owned by a team. The generator turns a valid manifest into a kafka-<team> skill. v1 supports SASL/SCRAM and MTLS (mutual TLS) for broker auth, plus Schema Registry basic auth; SASL/PLAIN (#63) and OAUTHBEARER (#65) are tracked separately.

team.description: Team identifier. Drives the generated skill name (kafka-<team>). Restricted to a path-safe character set so it can be used as a single path segment without escaping.

topics.description: Topics this team owns. Becomes the topic allowlist embedded in every generated script — scripts refuse to operate on names not in this list.

consumer_groups.description: Consumer groups this team owns. Becomes the group allowlist embedded in describe-group.sh, lag.sh, and reset-offsets.sh.

cluster.description: Cluster-wide shape. Per-environment values (broker addresses, credentials, cert paths) live in .env.<context> at runtime, not here.

auth.description: Authentication mechanism. v1 accepts SASL_SCRAM and MTLS; other values (SASL_PLAIN #63, OAUTHBEARER #65) are rejected with a pointer to the tracking issue.

tls.description: TLS posture for client connections. 'required' means the broker terminates TLS and the client must use it; 'none' is for cleartext SASL on a trusted network. Under auth: MTLS this MUST be 'required' — mutual TLS implies TLS — enforced by the top-level if/then/else below.

schema_registry.description: Optional Schema Registry block. Omit if the team's topics aren't schema-versioned. URL is supplied per-context via the .env file.

auth.description: Schema Registry auth. v1: 'basic' (HTTP Basic auth using CONTEXTS_<CTX>_SCHEMAREGISTRY_USERNAME and CONTEXTS_<CTX>_SCHEMAREGISTRY_PASSWORD per context, sent over HTTPS) or 'none' (anonymous, e.g. for a dev-only SR).

contexts.description: One entry per environment (dev / staging / prod / ...). Names appear verbatim in `kafkactl --context <name>` calls and as the uppercased + hyphen-to-underscore-normalized segment in `CONTEXTS_<NAME>_*` env vars. Per-`name` uniqueness is NOT expressible in JSON Schema draft 2020-12: uniqueItems only rejects fully-identical objects (so two contexts could share a `name` if their `sasl_mechanism` differs), and the env-var normalization means `dev-1` and `dev_1` collide on the same `CONTEXTS_DEV_1_*` prefix even though the raw strings differ. The generator validates uniqueness AFTER normalization (uppercase + `-` → `_`) before proceeding.

name.description: Context name. Must be a valid kafkactl context identifier and must round-trip through uppercasing for env-var lookup (so no leading digits, no dots).

sasl_mechanism.description: SCRAM variant used by this environment. Required when cluster.auth: SASL_SCRAM; forbidden when cluster.auth: MTLS (the cert is the auth — there's no SASL exchange).
